channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [KAMADGIRI] Kamadgiri Fashion: Price movements purely market-driven
item.description:
Kamadgiri Fashion Ltd responded to an email regarding price movements, stating that there is no additional or price-sensitive information that has not been disclosed to the stock exchange. The company clarified that the fluctuations in its share price are purely market-driven. It affirmed that all necessary disclosures pursuant to Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, have been made, and no material information that could impact the scrip's price/volume behavior has been withheld. Therefore, the company attributes the price movement solely to market conditions.

  • Company denies withholding price-sensitive information.
  • Share price movements are attributed to market forces.
  • All SEBI Regulation 30 disclosures have been made.
  • No material information impacting price/volume is withheld.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: KAMADGIRI FASHION LTD.-$ | Category: Clarification

item.title: [KRYPTONQ] Krypton Promoters Confirm No Undisclosed Share Encumbrances for FY26
item.description:
Krypton Industries Ltd has submitted its annual disclosure under Regulation 31(4) of SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011, for the financial year ended March 31, 2026. The disclosure, provided by the Promoter and Promoter Group, including Persons acting in concert, confirms that no additional encumbrances of shares, directly or indirectly, have been made beyond those already reported. This filing ensures regulatory compliance and transparency regarding the promoter group's shareholdings and any associated pledges or charges.

  • Krypton Industries filed annual disclosure under SEBI Takeover Regulations.
  • Promoters confirmed no new undisclosed share encumbrances for FY26.
  • Ensures compliance with Regulation 31(4) of SEBI SAST Regulations.
  • Provides transparency on promoter shareholding status and pledges.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: KRYPTON INDUSTRIES LTD. | Category: Shareholding

item.title: [VIKASECO] Vikas EcoTech receives ₹9.11 Cr Income Tax demand order
item.description:
Vikas EcoTech Ltd has received income tax demand orders from the Income Tax Department on March 27, 2026, totaling ₹9.11 Crore. These demands pertain to various assessment years and primarily stem from additions and/or disallowances made during assessment proceedings. The company is currently evaluating the orders and plans to pursue appropriate legal remedies, including filing appeals within the prescribed timelines. Based on a preliminary assessment, the company states there is no immediate material impact on its operations. The management, supported by legal advice, expresses confidence in achieving a favorable outcome at the appellate stage.

  • Vikas EcoTech received a ₹9.11 Cr income tax demand order.
  • The demand is for various assessment years due to disallowances.
  • Company plans to appeal the order and seek legal remedies.
  • No immediate material operational impact is expected.
  • Management is confident of a favorable appellate outcome.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Vikas EcoTech Ltd | Category: Tax & Penalty

item.title: [TARSONS] Tarsons Products Appoints Mani Kalyan Dusi as Head – Revenue
item.description:
Tarsons Products Ltd has announced the appointment of Mr. Mani Kalyan Dusi as Head – Revenue (India & APAC), designated as a Senior Management Personnel (SMP). The Board of Directors approved this appointment based on the Nomination & Remuneration Committee's recommendation. Mr. Dusi is expected to join the company on or before May 20, 2026. He brings over 18 years of extensive experience in the life sciences, pharmaceuticals, and academic sectors across the APAC region. His expertise includes commercial leadership, strategic marketing, go-to-market strategies, and P&L management, with a proven track record of driving business growth. He previously held key leadership roles at Merck Life Science and Thermo-Fisher Scientific.

  • Tarsons Products appointed Mr. Mani Kalyan Dusi as Head – Revenue (India & APAC).
  • Mr. Dusi is designated as a Senior Management Personnel (SMP).
  • He brings over 18 years of experience in life sciences and commercial leadership.
  • His expertise includes strategic marketing, GTM strategies, and P&L management.
  • He is expected to join the company on or before May 20, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Tarsons Products Ltd | Category: Management Change

item.title: [SCILAL] Shipping Corporation of India Land and Assets Ltd: Independent Director's Tenure Ends
item.description:
Shipping Corporation of India Land and Assets Limited announced the cessation of Prof. (Dr.) K. Jayaprasad as a Non-Official (Independent) Director on its Board. His one-year tenure, which commenced on April 15, 2025, concluded on April 14, 2026. The appointment was previously approved by the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet based on recommendations from the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways. This change is in compliance with Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

  • Independent Director Prof. K. Jayaprasad's tenure ended.
  • His one-year term concluded on April 14, 2026.
  • Cessation is due to the completion of the approved tenure.
  • The change is compliant with SEBI Listing Regulations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Shipping Corporation of India Land and Assets Ltd | Category: Management Change

item.title: [INNOVACAP] Innova Captab: ₹15.81 Cr CGST Demand Dropped, No Liability
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Innova Captab Ltd received a Show Cause cum Demand Notice dated October 15, 2025, under section 74 of the CGST Act, 2017, from the Central Goods & Services Tax Commissionerate, Shimla, proposing a demand of ₹15,81,37,255.40. The company filed a timely response with supporting documents. After reviewing the submissions, the adjudicating authority issued an Order-in-Original on April 14, 2026, dropping the proceedings. The authority held that the matter raised in the notice was legally sustainable, resulting in the proposed demand and penal proceedings being dropped, with no liability for the company.

  • Company received a Show Cause cum Demand Notice for ₹15.81 Cr.
  • Notice was issued under section 74 of the CGST Act, 2017.
  • Company successfully challenged the tax demand with its response.
  • Adjudicating authority dropped the entire demand and penal proceedings.
  • No tax liability of ₹15.81 Cr for Innova Captab Ltd.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Innova Captab Ltd | Category: Tax & Penalty

item.title: [VGIL] Veer Global EGM Notice Corrigendum: Preferential Issue Details Added
item.description:
Veer Global Infraconstruction Ltd issued a corrigendum to its Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) notice dated March 30, 2026, scheduled for April 25, 2026. The correction follows observations from BSE regarding the company's application for in-principle approval for a proposed preferential issue of equity shares. The corrigendum adds details to the Explanatory Statement concerning the conversion of outstanding unsecured loans from Veerone Limited and Veer Finance Limited into equity shares. Specifically, ₹1.80 crore from Veerone Limited and ₹5.00 crore from Veer Finance Limited, totaling ₹6.80 crore, are proposed to be set off against equity shares. Additionally, a typographical error in the resolution regarding the number of shares allotted to respective allottees has been corrected, while the total shares, consideration, and post-allotment paid-up capital remain unchanged.

  • Veer Global issued a corrigendum to its EGM notice for April 25, 2026.
  • Corrigendum adds details for a proposed preferential issue of equity shares.
  • ₹6.80 crore in unsecured loans to be converted into equity shares.
  • Loans from Veerone Ltd (₹1.80 Cr) and Veer Finance Ltd (₹5.00 Cr) are involved.
  • Typographical error in the number of shares for allottees was corrected.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Veer Global Infraconstruction Ltd | Category: AGM/EGM

item.title: [BLUBLND-B] Blue Blends Board to meet on April 17, 2026, for Q1, H1, Q3 FY26 results
item.description:
Blue Blends (India) Ltd has announced that a meeting of its Board of Directors is scheduled for Friday, April 17, 2026. The primary agenda for this meeting is to consider, approve, and take on record the Unaudited Standalone and Consolidated Financial Results of the Company. The results to be reviewed include those for the quarter ended June 30, 2025, the quarter and half year ended September 30, 2025, and the quarter ended December 31, 2025. This disclosure is made in compliance with Regulation 29 and 33 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

  • Board meeting scheduled for April 17, 2026.
  • To consider unaudited financial results for three quarters.
  • Results for Q1, H1, and Q3 FY26 to be approved.
  • Meeting is in compliance with SEBI Listing Regulations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Blue Blends (India) Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [MOTHERSON] Motherson dissolves SMR-Jersey; no impact on consolidated net worth
item.description:
Samvardhana Motherson International Ltd's indirect wholly-owned subsidiary, Samvardhana Motherson Reflectec Group Holdings Limited (SMR-Jersey), has been voluntarily dissolved by the Commercial Register, effective April 10, 2026. This action was taken to simplify the group holding structure. For FY 2024-25, SMR-Jersey contributed Euro 7.27 million (0.00%) to consolidated revenue and Euro 161.85 million (4.62%) to consolidated net worth. Critically, prior to dissolution, SMR-Jersey's net worth was distributed to Motherson Global Investments B.V., another consolidated entity, ensuring no impact on the company's overall consolidated net worth.

  • Samvardhana Motherson's indirect subsidiary, SMR-Jersey, has been voluntarily dissolved.
  • The dissolution aims to simplify the group holding structure.
  • SMR-Jersey's income was negligible to consolidated revenue in FY25.
  • Its net worth was 4.62% of consolidated net worth in FY25.
  • The dissolution has no impact on the company's consolidated net worth.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Samvardhana Motherson International Ltd | Category: Subsidiary

item.title: [NECCLTD] NECC to issue 45 lakh equity shares via preferential issue to promoter
item.description:
North Eastern Carrying Corporation Ltd (NECC) has proposed to issue up to 45,00,000 equity shares on a preferential basis to its promoter, Mr. Sunil Kumar Jain. The shares, with a face value of ₹10 each, will be issued at a price of ₹14.91 per equity share, including a premium of ₹4.91. This issuance is intended for the conversion of outstanding unsecured loans into equity, subject to the approval of the company's shareholders. The issue price will be adjusted against the outstanding loan amount upon allotment.

  • NECC proposes a preferential issue of equity shares to its promoter.
  • Up to 45 lakh equity shares will be issued at ₹14.91 each.
  • The issuance is for converting outstanding unsecured loans into equity.
  • The preferential issue is subject to shareholder approval.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: NORTH EASTERN CARRYING CORPORATION LTD. | Category: Corporate Action
